name: playground-cinematic-bloom
description: Cinematic profile + tree scenario — verifies the Wave 1 bloom path. Cinematic enables fx.post-process (bloom) + render.penumbra-backdrop, but the playground viewport doesn't bind Penumbra (Phase D defer), so this captures bloom-only without Penumbra interop concerns. Used by humans + me (Claude) to eyeball whether bloom is visible. Pixel-diff tolerance is high — the point is visual confirmation, not regression locking.

setup.vlmDescription: Debug Playground overlay with 'Tree' pill highlighted. Cinematic profile is active so fx.post-process (bloom) is enabled. The 3D scene shows ~121 tree-arranged blue spherical nodes against a dark background. Critically — each node should have a visible soft halo of bloom around it (additive light leak suggesting a glow), not just a flat sphere. The dark background should NOT be pitch black; the baseline emissive (Wave 1 phase E / Bug 0019 fix) gives nodes self-illumination so the scene reads as a constellation, not voids around lit objects.
